texts.name: Gold
texts.flavorDesc: Used across all inhabited worlds by the merchants of the 7 Circles Guild, this currency is officially called the "Gyre". But everyone just calls it "gold".

texts.name: Craft point
texts.flavorDesc: These tokens rewarding good craftsmanship were first used among the Silkworm Fellowship of artisans, and ended up spreading throughout Siagarta.

texts.desc:
Gain [Experience] thorugh various tasks such as hunting monsters, gathering, exploring or crafting.
Hit a certain threshold to reach the next [Level] and gain power.
texts.name: Experience
Cheese Moon
texts.flavorDesc: Forged in the depths of Irukal, this axe was designed by a people who had never seen a moon in their life, and it shows.

texts.name: Amon Ram, the Creator
texts.flavorDesc: Amon, ram-headed god of creation, appeared to Lady Skover in a vision. No one really believed her story at the time, but it did make the Rams appear more legitimate.

texts.name: Light Practice Sword
texts.flavorDesc: If you've been given this sword, someone thinks you can't be trusted with a real one.

texts.name: Glory
texts.flavorDesc: To conquer without risk is to triumph without glory. Thankfully, going to battle with that sword makes such a situation very unlikely.
There is a metaphor at play here: you can see through the blade, but the blade can cut through you. This is the kind of poetry the Apix clan enjoys.
texts.name: Diaphanous Mokshi
Whispers
texts.flavorDesc: These maces are so heavy they produce a terrifying sound as they sunder the air, which is usually the last sound one can hear.

texts.name: Rough Shield
texts.flavorDesc: This shield is so rudimentary it usually ends up doing more harm than it prevents.

texts.name: Dominion
texts.flavorDesc: The best defence is sometimes believed to be attack. Whoever said that has never been hit in the face with a steel shield.

texts.name: Magma Mia
texts.flavorDesc: According to legend, this is an overcooked slice of pizza from the Primeval Age. Some scholars pretend it is just cheese. Either way, no one ever managed to eat it.

texts.name: Crabgantua's Kneecap
texts.flavorDesc: A creature with three pairs of legs should have more than one kneecap. Crabgantua only had one, but its size and hardness is admirable.
The sudden evolution of the bees' appearance triggered a sort of identity crisis, after which they started making all their stuff look like wings.
texts.name: Wingsabers
Twin Pillars of Justice
texts.flavorDesc: These weapons have little to do with justice and they certainly aren't pillars, but the Crimson Rams have a taste for metaphors.

texts.name: Iron Fins of the Leviathan
texts.flavorDesc: Having fins of iron is not necessarily an advantage. In fact, it made the Leviathan slow enough for the fishermen to harvest its precious fins.

texts.name: Rusty Knives
texts.flavorDesc: These training knives were confiscated from their previous bearer for obvious reasons.

texts.name: Twin Fangs of Ratsar
texts.flavorDesc: Like most rodents, Ratsar doesn't have fangs, so he had to steal these. Someday, another toothless creature will probably take them from him too.
At the end of a warrior's arms, these claws look like the gluttonous mouths of two giant snakes.
texts.name: Clawdius
Ramulus & Ramus
texts.flavorDesc: The purpose of ram horns is to be struck against other horns. For this reason, rams are often represented as twins, so they can strike each other's horns and find purpose in the process.

texts.name: Martyr of Enripit
texts.flavorDesc: Enripit, once known as the Nightlord who almost stopped the Guardians War, is no longer even able to stop the Kobolds from stealing his sword.

texts.name: Skelmane
texts.flavorDesc: Through the centuries, none of the legendary Kobold smiths was ever able to explain how Skelmane was forged, or to identify its source.

texts.name: Judgement
texts.flavorDesc: The institutions of justice are rather uncomplicated in Siagarta. This single axe, for instance, cumulates legislative, judiciary and executive power.

texts.name: Pet Bees of the Pheromonarch
texts.flavorDesc: It's unclear why these specific bees never evolved like their humanoid sisters, but owning one of these tiny hives has become a status symbol for Apix royalty.

texts.name: Lady Bee’s Ceremonial Stinger
texts.flavorDesc: In the past, Apix princesses ennobled royal guards using their own stingers, but a series of incidents convinced them to use that spear instead.

texts.name: Gorgon Ratsay’s Toothpick
texts.flavorDesc: Although the legendary Ratsay used his spear to hunt, cook and eat, this tool was mostly remembered as the first toothpick in Kobold history.

texts.name: Radiance
texts.flavorDesc: Like a sunbeam hitting the dewy blossoms of the morning, this long wooden beam descends vigorously upon the skulls and noses of the unworthy.
The Crimson Rams are known for burning things: heretic books, heretic pagans... and incense. In scorching burners they often use as blunt weapons against heretics.
texts.name: Incense Burner of the Pastor

texts.flavorDesc: Auster the parrot was the strategic advisor of Admiral Aquilon. After the bird's passing, she replaced him with this effigy, which failed to provide the same insights.
texts.name: Auster's Effigy

texts.flavorDesc: A bow is an object of faith and each arrow is a mystery. Will it hit its target? Will its target hit back? Is there still time for a second shot?
texts.name: Credence

texts.flavorDesc: This weapon is a testimony to the Rams' lack of imagination. If something can hurt them, they always picture it wearing a pair of horns.
texts.name: Horns of the Wind
Apprentice's Grimoire
texts.flavorDesc: At one point, this was an Expert's Grimoire, but all the advanced pages are damaged beyond recognition.

texts.name: Book of Mi'Mizan
texts.flavorDesc: Mizan is known for travelling around Merops to learn Naya magic from the Nepsids. No one knows what he did there, but they're no longer taking apprentices.

texts.name: Ghost Clams of the Low Tide
texts.flavorDesc: Before Lord Rulean stole this artifact from the Nepsids, they thought it was responsible for the tides. Luckily, the theft proved that it was in fact useless.

texts.name: Rehearsal Scepter
texts.flavorDesc: This false scepter is materially identical to a real one. But material reality is of little importance when it comes to rituals.

texts.name: Flame of Argol
texts.flavorDesc: Burning since before the war of the Four Hundred, this candle is widely regarded as the living soul of the legendary Argol Konda.

texts.name: Revived Argol
texts.flavorDesc: A fire cannot grow unless it is fueled. One can only wonder what sacrifices allowed this burning soul to become so ardent.

texts.name: Ipheion, Star Blossom
texts.flavorDesc: Starflowers are pretty common, but few of them are shooting stars. And even fewer can cut an armor in half.
These pearls are said to originate from the night sky, where they were plucked by the Agias themselves.
texts.name: Hollow Pearls of Uraneps
Thornlace
texts.flavorDesc: A masterpiece of Apix craft, Thornlace is also an expression of the clan's philosophy: beauty is a warning, always laced with danger.

texts.name: White Shirt
texts.flavorDesc: There are two things this shirt cannot protect you from: any kind of damage, and a farmer's tan.

texts.name: Blue Shirt
texts.flavorDesc: There are two things this shirt cannot protect you from: any kind of damage, and a farmer's tan.

texts.name: Fin Armor
texts.flavorDesc: According to legend, a maiden crafted this funeral garment from the remains of the bloodthirsty monster their lover slayed at the cost of their life.

texts.name: Emblem of the Perpetual Fondue
texts.flavorDesc: To the Kobolds, the Perpetual Fondue is not just a symbol of vitality: it is the essence of life itself. Warm, eternal, delicious, and flowing like the bloodstream of an entire people.

texts.name: Cantal Goya's Breastplate
texts.flavorDesc: Former priestess of the Fondue, Goya was a founding member of the Emmen Tunnel order which advocates for the exploitation of Siagarta's underground.

texts.name: Rosapollina's Bejeweled Breastplate
texts.flavorDesc: Rosapollina was the first Apix Pollincess to perish while trying to fly into the sun. The heaviness of this armor probably didn't help her achieve this feat.

texts.name: Cinderelhive's Bejeweled Breastplate
texts.flavorDesc: Cinderelhive was the first Apix Pollincess to be devoured by a Pumpkin Seedling. The clan has since renounced pollinating the fields of Azuram.

texts.name: Jacket of the Last Pirate
texts.flavorDesc: In the early days of the Chrysalis Age, pirate clans saw the desertion of Siagarta as an opportunity. The Nepsids soon showed them how wrong they were.

texts.name: Emblem of Radiant Nectar
texts.flavorDesc: It is a well-known fact: nectar is a result of sunlight, not a source of it. But this is not how symbolism works. In Art, the tastiest element is always at the center of everything.

texts.name: Charm of the Fisher King
texts.flavorDesc: That seashell is too small to protect a human chest, but it sends a message. The nature of the message, however, remains unclear.

texts.name: Stinger Doublet
texts.flavorDesc: Having your main weapon attached to your butt is inconvenient. All bees know that. Having a stinger on your chest wouldn't be much better, but this garment proves it would be more intimidating.

texts.name: Goldilock's Thrice Latched Jacket
texts.flavorDesc: According to legend, this vest once belonged to a very prosperous and gluttonous Kobold whose ever-expanding belly could only be contained with an ever-increasing number of buttons.

texts.name: Casual Clothes of the Pollincess
texts.flavorDesc: Pollincesses are rarely seen without armor. On such occasions, they are somehow even more dangerous.

texts.name: Vesture of the Mussel Hunter
texts.flavorDesc: As the Nepsids say, “if a pair of pants is good enough for fishing, it’s good enough for battle.” Of course, in a world where salmon wear pants, fishing and battle are usually the same thing.

texts.name: Flapping Trousers of Confusion
texts.flavorDesc: Flapping trousers are not as useful as flapping wings, but they can confuse enemies. They can also confuse allies, though. They can confuse anyone, including their bearer.

texts.name: Lead Lined Trousers
texts.flavorDesc: Whether a pound of lead is heavier than a pound of feathers is irrelevant. To craft a pair of pants that is both resistant and comfortable, you need both anyway.

texts.name: Flying Trousers Prototype
texts.flavorDesc: The research on flying trousers was abandoned after this last prototype. Which makes it a very rare and especially useless item.

texts.name: Sir Rulean's Fishing Pants
texts.flavorDesc: When Rulean embarked on his ill-fated diplomatic journey in the depths of Merops, he was so well received at first that the Nepsid court had these shorts tailored for him.

texts.name: Nepicur's Vacation Shorts
texts.flavorDesc: Nepicur has always enjoyed life to the fullest: waging horrible wars, fishing entire oceans to depletion, and losing their shorts at sea on holidays.

texts.name: Diluvian Kelp Shorts
texts.flavorDesc: When the Primeval Deluge almost eradicated all terrestrial beings across the nine worlds, each of them had to develop a survival strategy. This is when humankind invented the shorts.

texts.name: Breeches of the Twin Stings
texts.flavorDesc: Bees can only have one stinger, but their prey does not know that. They have seen the Apix warriors mutate so many times that they are ready to believe pretty much anything.

texts.name: Garment of the Aurock Master
texts.flavorDesc: As the principal purveyor of source material for the Perpetual Fondue, the Aurock Master is a crucial contributor to Kobold society. Accordingly, their shorts are held in high regard.

texts.name: Wrong Trousers
texts.flavorDesc: One would argue that few trousers feel exactly right, but it is almost universally recognized that these don't.

texts.name: Zenobee's Breeches
texts.flavorDesc: From triumph to exile, Queen Zenobee has been the cause or victim of many changes. But one thing always remained stable and trustworthy throughout her life: these breeches.

texts.name: Rat-Vachol's Patched Up Pants
texts.flavorDesc: Penniless, freethinking and a frequent user of explosives, Rat-Vachol had every reason to wear damaged pants. This pair survived, however, much longer than he did.

texts.name: High-speed Clamdiggers
texts.flavorDesc: The negative impact of pants on swimming speed is the reason most fish don't wear any. Speed remains a crucial factor for those who do, as demonstrated by this impressive pair.

texts.name: Layered Greaves of the Royal Guard
texts.flavorDesc: Imitating the articulate layers of a hymenoptera's abdomen, these greaves are as flexible as they are resistant.

texts.name: Baby teeth of the Leviathan
texts.flavorDesc: There is only one Leviathan, and it lost its last baby teeth before the Primeval Age, but it had so many of them it's not that rare to come across one.

texts.name: Pride of the Harpooner
texts.flavorDesc: You'd think the pride of a harpooner would be their harpoon. That would be a mistake. People don't usually throw their treasures into the sea like they do with harpoons.

texts.name: Buskins of Essential Emptiness
texts.flavorDesc: Much like the holes in a cheese wheel, caves are what makes life possible in the underworld. For this reason, emptiness is regarded by Kobolds as the founding principle of all things.

texts.name: Boots of Abyssal Essence
texts.flavorDesc: This leather smells like it was never supposed to leave the ocean.

texts.name: Melain's Golden Greaves
texts.flavorDesc: Princess Melain was originally quite pleased with these greaves, but had them replaced when she found out how weak gold actually is.

texts.name: Will of the Spelunker
texts.flavorDesc: The first human travelers who explored the underworlds were surprisingly determined to find hell. They did find death, but the exact location of hell remains a mystery.

texts.name: Spelunking Shoes of Emmen Tunnel
texts.flavorDesc: The Kobold doctrine of Emmen Tunnel essentially promotes the exhaustive exploration of Siagarta's undergrounds, which makes decent shoes more than needed.

texts.name: Corycia's Nimble Sabatons
texts.flavorDesc: Most shoes connect their bearer to the ground, but these sabatons have nothing to do with the ground. They free their bearer from it, connecting them with the air instead.

texts.name: Spare Sandals of Young Nephisto
texts.flavorDesc: Emperor Nephisto never wore these sandals, but they were made for him during his teenage years, and have been prized by collectors ever since.

texts.name: Cleo's Ethereal Blossoms
texts.flavorDesc: Ephemerality is a sad but inevitable character of all flowers. Fortunately, the Apix clan managed to subvert this rule by crafting flowers out of leather.

texts.name: Gold Pouches
texts.flavorDesc: Storing gold in your shoes might make sense when you don't want thieves or customs officers to notice you, but using coin purses as buskins will reduce your chances of success.

texts.name: Palm of the Lagoon
texts.flavorDesc: Their protective layers may look like duck beaks, but no ducks were harmed to manufacture these gloves. Many other animals were harmed, though.

texts.name: Faith of the Spelunker
texts.flavorDesc: Unprepared against the various gases that fill its galleries, Irukal's explorers have often experienced hallucinations. These gloves offer no protection against such woes.

texts.name: Gauntlets of the Royal Guard
texts.flavorDesc: If a monarch is well protected, their gauntlets are seldom used. Therefore, it is wiser to invest in good gauntlets for their guards, who have more opportunities to throw punches.

texts.name: Gauntlets of the Pheromonarch
texts.flavorDesc: Through the years, as the Apix clan grew increasingly similar to humanoid species, the term "Pheromonarch" fell into disuse. Much like these gauntlets.

texts.name: Heraclette's Brawling Gauntlets
texts.flavorDesc: Heraclette never wears gauntlets. They lost this pair many years ago and did not notice. It is not even their size.

texts.name: Vambraces of the Swarm
texts.flavorDesc: This perfectly balanced equipment is protective enough for warfare, lightweight enough for long flights and open enough to be worn while harvesting pollen.

texts.name: Featherlight Hand Wraps of the Exile
Featherlight Hand Wraps of the Trespasser
Burden of the Abyss
texts.flavorDesc: These heavy training gloves were crafted for a legendary Nepsid warrior whose name was eventually forgotten after he sunk into the abyss. No one knows how his gloves resurfaced.

texts.name: Infused Hand Wraps of the Exile
Infused Hand Wraps of the Trespasser
Ethereal Hand Wraps of the Exile
Ethereal Hand Wraps of the Trespasser
Palmaryllis
texts.flavorDesc: Delicate, pastoral, and perfect for hiding playing cards or lethal weapons. These gloves are everything an Apix royal needs.

texts.name: Ceremonial Siren Gloves
texts.flavorDesc: Mermaids have normal hands. They don’t need a special pair of gloves. But that doesn’t mean they shouldn’t have one.

texts.name: Reblochonk's Working Gloves
texts.flavorDesc: Such a glove offers minimal protection and prevents their bearer from closing their hand correctly. On the other hand, they are also wearing a similar glove

texts.name: Unity of the Thirty Kingdoms
texts.flavorDesc: There are many kingdoms in Irukal, with different systems of values, organization and culture. These differences are made trivial by the fact that they share the Perpetual Fondue.

texts.name: Aura of the Honeycomb
texts.flavorDesc: This small honeycomb carved in amber served to remind its bearer of where she came from. Of course, now it is only a reminder that you killed this poor bee.

texts.name: Standardized Kobold Belt
texts.flavorDesc: In Siagarta, not all heroes wear capes, but all Kobolds wear belts, and they mostly look like this one.

texts.name: Cheese Rolling Champion's Belt
texts.flavorDesc: Cheese rolling is a dangerous sport, but it becomes even more dangerous once you put on this blunt, heavy belt.

texts.name: Relic of the Four Hundred
texts.flavorDesc: A possible explanation for the short-lived reign of the Four Hundred in Irukal is their adoration of the sun. In a world without a sky, this cult proved a bit too discouraging.

texts.name: Propolis Heart of Apix
texts.flavorDesc: While human alchemists were wasting centuries trying to produce aether, the bees produced unbelievable quantities of propolis, which is arguably much more useful.

texts.name: Seahorse Leather Belt
texts.flavorDesc: Seahorse leather is not as thick or as durable as cow leather. It’s not especially delicate either. And it stinks. On the other hand, it is a lot more expensive.

texts.name: Knotr'Edam
texts.flavorDesc: Popular among round-bellied clerics, this belt is doubly sealed by a wheel-shaped buckle and a devoutly tightened knot.

texts.name: Ceremonial Nepsid Belt
texts.flavorDesc: Nepsid oracles only wear that kind of belt on special occasions. If things get really serious, they even wear it together with a pair of pants.

texts.name: Buzz Lightweight's Sylvan Harness
texts.flavorDesc: This prototype was developed when the Apix clan first tried putting warriors in honey cannons. No similar equipment has been produced ever since.

texts.name: Eye of Merops
texts.flavorDesc: This legendary pearl was stolen so many times it slowly earned the reputation of attracting bad luck, which lowered its value considerably.

texts.name: Brie von de Cape
texts.flavorDesc: Cheese is not an appropriate tailoring material. This fact will not prevent the Kobolds from trying, as exemplified by this garment.

texts.name: Reinforced Neck Gaiter of the Exile
Reinforced Neck Gaiter of the Trespasser
Fugu Zela's Armored Cape
texts.flavorDesc: Fugu was part of a famous rebel group that threatened Nephisto's power over Merops. His war scream was so loud he could fend off entire regiments without having to fight.

texts.name: Condensed Neck Gaiter of the Exile
Condensed Neck Gaiter of the Trespasser
Veil of the Royal Nymph
texts.flavorDesc: Nymphs do not have any wings, fur or stripes. Deeming this bareness unworthy of a monarch, the Apix clan crafted this supreme garment which was passed down from queen to queen.

texts.name: Veil of the Royal Nymph
texts.flavorDesc: Nymphs do not have any wings, fur or stripes. Deeming this bareness unworthy of a monarch, the Apix clan crafted this supreme garment which was passed down from queen to queen.

texts.name: Protected Neck Gaiter of the Exile
Protected Neck Gaiter of the Trespasser
Jenny Hanibal's Armored Cape
texts.flavorDesc: Jenny was part of a famous rebel group that threatened Nephisto's power over Merops. She was so old she looked like a fantastical creature, which greatly intimidated her enemies.

texts.name: Blessed Neck Gaiter of the Exile
Blessed Neck Gaiter of the Trespasser
Reversible Bib of the Cheese Taster
texts.flavorDesc: A cheese taster should always wear adequate protection, but they should also be prepared to run in case of an emergency call. Thankfully, this bib can become a cape in a fraction of seconds.

texts.name: Flight of the Rumblebee
texts.flavorDesc: A rumblebee is to the bumblebee what thunder is to gurgling. Which makes little difference, as both species were eradicated by the Apix clan.

texts.name: Red Scarf of Raklepto the Maniac
texts.flavorDesc: Most thieves try to keep a low profile. Raklepto, on the contrary, seemed to pride themselves on getting noticed and still managing to escape. Until they did not.

texts.name: Mystical Placemat of the Gourmet
texts.flavorDesc: The Gourmet was a sorcerer capable of channeling the magical power of cheese. This legend, however, fails to describe any concrete manifestation of such a power.

texts.name: Wings of the Prophet
texts.flavorDesc: The mission of a prophet is to be remembered. Sadly, that cape is the only legacy of the nameless predicator who once bore it.

texts.name: Veil of the Royal Nymph
texts.flavorDesc: Nymphs do not have any wings, fur or stripes. Deeming this bareness unworthy of a monarch, the Apix clan crafted this supreme garment which was passed down from queen to queen.

texts.name: Circle of Precision
texts.flavorDesc: This ring is purported to grant exceptional fighting abilities. Some scholars suggest it merely grants style, which in turn grants confidence, which makes adventurers fight better. In any case, the result is the same.

texts.name: Circle of Zeal
texts.flavorDesc: This ring is purported to grant exceptional fighting abilities. Some scholars suggest it merely grants style, which in turn grants confidence, which makes adventurers fight better. In any case, the result is the same.

texts.name: Circle of Fracture
texts.flavorDesc: This ring is purported to grant exceptional fighting abilities. Some scholars suggest it merely grants style, which in turn grants confidence, which makes adventurers fight better. In any case, the result is the same.

texts.name: Circle of Clarity
texts.flavorDesc: This ring is purported to grant exceptional fighting abilities. Some scholars suggest it merely grants style, which in turn grants confidence, which makes adventurers fight better. In any case, the result is the same.

texts.name: Pendant of Versatility
texts.flavorDesc: This prodigious amulet is perfectly balanced. It has the ability to brush off all of its bearer’s shortcomings, at the exception of indecisiveness.

texts.name: Stone of Power
texts.flavorDesc: For unknown reasons, wearing this stone as a pendant is more effective than ingesting it. It is also much less dangerous.
texts.desc: ::ref_desc::

texts.name: Philosopher's Stone
texts.flavorDesc: This marvel is the unattainable dream of all alchemists. Philosophers don't care much about rocks, though. No one knows where this name comes from.

texts.name: Azuramean Owl
texts.desc: Glide as long as you want.
texts.flavorDesc: In the rocky grasslands of Azuram, the owls grew so big they started abducting human travelers. Eventually, the travelers got used to it and started using these animals as a means of transport.

Skoverial Raccoon
texts.desc: Glide as long as you want.
texts.flavorDesc: On the ethereal grounds that soared above Skover, many animal species had to quickly adapt to a newly aerial environment. In other words, raccoons can fly now.

texts.name: Brown Skoverial Raccoon
texts.desc: Glide as long as you want.
texts.flavorDesc: On the ethereal grounds that soared above Skover, many animal species had to quickly adapt to a newly aerial environment. In other words, raccoons can fly now.

texts.name: Green Dragoon
texts.flavorDesc: High above Skover’s floating fields, the local creatures had to adjust fast to life in the sky. That’s how the glider-drakes came to be small, friendly dragon pets with soft wings and an easy glide. They flit between the hovering cliffs like playful paper kites, happy to follow anyone who offers a warm hand and a little adventure.

texts.name: Mortar
texts.desc: Used by [Alchemist] to craft potions and elixirs.

texts.name: Pliers
texts.desc: Used by [Jeweller] to craft jewellry.

texts.name: Knife
texts.desc: Used by [Cook] to cook delicious dishes.

texts.name: Copper Ore
texts.flavorDesc: This metal is rather soft, which makes it easy to shape into ineffective armors and disappointing tools.
texts.desc: Gathered from a deposit, this ore can be used by [Blacksmith]s and [Jeweller]s.

texts.name: Copper Ingot
texts.desc: Can be used by [Blacksmith]s to craft gear.
texts.flavorDesc: This metal is rather soft, which makes it easy to shape into ineffective armors and disappointing tools.

texts.name: Tin Ore
texts.desc: Gathered from a deposit, this ore can be used by [Blacksmith]s and [Jeweller]s.
texts.flavorDesc: Despite its humble characteristics, tin is appreciated for its ability to combine well with other metals. It’s the teamplayer of smithery.

texts.name: Bronze Ingot
texts.flavorDesc: When smiths discovered that combining a bad metal with a worse one could result in something good, it was a great day for both technology and philosophy.
texts.desc: Can be used by [Blacksmith]s to craft gear.

texts.name: Tungstene Ore
texts.desc: Gathered from a deposit, this ore can be used by [Blacksmith]s and [Jeweller]s.
texts.flavorDesc: This metal is pretty hard and heat resistant. This is good news unless you're the one mining it, forging it or fighting someone whose gear is made of it.

texts.name: Tungstene Ingot
texts.desc: Can be used by [Blacksmith]s to craft gear.
texts.flavorDesc: This metal is pretty hard and heat resistant. This is good news unless you're the one mining it, forging it or fighting someone whose gear is made of it.

texts.name: Shimmering Alloy
texts.desc: A highly resistant alloy favored by [Blacksmith]s to craft armor reinforcements.
texts.flavorDesc: This radiant metal looks like liquid light. Thankfully, it is not liquid at all in normal fighting circumstances.

texts.name: Copper Prospecting
texts.desc: Grind some ores to find basic precious stones.

texts.name: Copper Setting
texts.desc: A piece of copper used by [Jeweller]s to craft gear.
texts.flavorDesc: The transformation of this metal into something useful has begun. But it could still go wrong.

texts.name: Cracked Rock
texts.desc: Usually found on rocky creatures, it can be used by [Blacksmith]s and [Jeweller]s.
texts.flavorDesc: Regular rocks are pretty much worthless. But cracked rocks are a whole other story.

texts.name: Cut Stone
texts.desc: Used by [Jeweller]s to polish precious gems.
texts.flavorDesc: It takes a cheap stone to polish a precious one.

texts.name: Fine Dust
texts.desc: Pas encore assigner a un job

texts.name: Glittering Stone
texts.desc: A rare stone that can be used by [Blacksmith]s and [Jeweller]s.
texts.flavorDesc: All that glitters isn't gold.. Which is good. This stone, for example, is worth about 15 gold coins.

texts.name: Simple Pearl
texts.desc: Usually found on aquatic creatures, it can be used in various jobs.
texts.flavorDesc: One creature's kidney stone is another creature's treasure.

texts.name: Amber
texts.flavorDesc: Note to all mineralogists: if there's a beetle inside, it's not topaz.
texts.desc: [Jeweller]s can cut this raw crystal into a gemstone.

texts.name: Beryl
texts.flavorDesc: According to legend, only the warmth of this crystal's glint can wake up the slumber princess: Beryl Sleep.
texts.desc: [Jeweller]s can cut this raw crystal into a gemstone.

texts.name: Cut Amber
texts.desc: A perfectly cut gemstone used by [Jeweller]s to craft powerful items.
texts.flavorDesc: Note to all mineralogists: if there's a beetle inside, it's not topaz.

texts.name: Cut Beryl
texts.desc: A perfectly cut gemstone used by [Jeweller]s to craft powerful items.
texts.flavorDesc: According to legend, only the warmth of this crystal's glint can wake up the slumber princess: Beryl Sleep.

texts.name: Hunter's Sauce
texts.desc: Used by [Cook]s to season dishes.
texts.flavorDesc: As the great Kobold chef Gorgon Ratsay once said: "Ah haff no idea what's in zat soss."

texts.name: Fisherman's Sauce
texts.desc: Used by [Cook]s to season dishes.
texts.flavorDesc: The secret to a good meal is a good sauce. That's what Mackerevelli said after eating nothing but sauce when he was exiled on a remote island.

texts.name: Herbes de Skover
texts.desc: Used by [Cook]s to create seasoning.
texts.flavorDesc: Across Siagarta, only 10% of all aromatics branded under this name are actually produced in the Skover region.

texts.name: Mayple Wood
texts.desc: Sold by merchants, this material can be used in many different crafts.
texts.flavorDesc: This soft, versatile essence of wood is especially easy to sculpt, which means it is also easy to deteriorate.

texts.name: Madrigold Petal
texts.flavorDesc: As the wind passes through the madrigold's horn, it sings a delicate song that poets admire.
texts.desc: Can be used by [Alchemist]s and [Cook]s.

texts.name: Lavendula Petal
texts.desc: Can be used by [Alchemist]s and [Cook]s.
texts.flavorDesc: This plant can heal almost any affliction. If consumed by a healthy person, however, it becomes extremely dangerous.

texts.name: Ancient Thyme Petal
texts.desc: Used by [Cook]s to season dishes.
texts.flavorDesc: As far as human memory goes, this plant has always been called ancient. No one can tell if it was ever young.

texts.name: Zealotus Petal
texts.desc: Can be used by [Alchemist]s.
texts.flavorDesc: The original name of the Zealotus was gradually forgotten when people started using this pun to mock the Crimson Rams. Now, the Rams themselves use the name.

texts.name: Fresh Flower Powder
texts.desc: Used by [Alchemist]s to craft potions and elixirs.
texts.flavorDesc: Alchemists are insensitive creatures. Show them the most delicate manifestation of nature's beauty, and they'll pulverize it to make hair lotion.

texts.name: Fragrant Flower Powder
texts.flavorDesc: Alchemists are insensitive creatures. Show them the most delicate manifestation of nature's beauty, and they'll pulverize it to make hair lotion.

texts.name: Refined Flower Powder
texts.flavorDesc: Alchemists are insensitive creatures. Show them the most delicate manifestation of nature's beauty, and they'll pulverize it to make hair lotion.

texts.name: Silken Flower Powder
texts.flavorDesc: Alchemists are insensitive creatures. Show them the most delicate manifestation of nature's beauty, and they'll pulverize it to make hair lotion.

texts.name: Ethereal Flower Powder
texts.flavorDesc: Alchemists are insensitive creatures. Show them the most delicate manifestation of nature's beauty, and they'll pulverize it to make hair lotion.

texts.name: Strange Spores
texts.desc: Can be used by [Alchemist]s.
texts.flavorDesc: All spores are kind of strange. Applied to spores, the word "strange" sounds more like "suspect". These spores are probably illegal.

texts.name: Subtle Essence
texts.desc: Used by [Alchemist]s.
texts.flavorDesc: In alchemist jargon, "subtle" means it has no taste, smell, weight, materiality or proven properties. It barely exists. They love it.

texts.name: Linen
texts.desc: Usually found on humanoid creatures, it can be used by [Outfitter]s.
texts.flavorDesc: Linen clothing is favored by sprightly adventurers for its strength, and by wounded adventurers for its absorbency.

texts.name: Light Leather Strap
texts.desc: Usually found on wild animals, it can be used by [Outfitter]s and [Blacksmith]s.
texts.flavorDesc: Widely used by the Pilgrims to fasten their transportation devices, straps also allow the Crimson Rams to keep the same pants after weeks of fasting.

texts.name: Linen Reel
texts.desc: Can be used by [Outfitter]s to craft gear.
texts.flavorDesc: This tiny reel of thread could turn into anything. A shirt, a pair of trousers, a cat's short-lived toy or a very bad fishing line.

texts.name: Tanned Leather
texts.desc: Can be used by [Outfitter]s to craft gear.
texts.flavorDesc: A competent tanner could craft beautiful garments out of this, but for now it's just gross.

texts.name: Soft Fur
texts.desc: A rare fur from a wild animal that can be used by [Outfitter]s and [Blacksmith]s.
texts.flavorDesc: It's a matter of debate whether humans started hunting for fur because they didn't have enough, or if they stopped growing fur because they started wearing it.

texts.name: Hidebound Weave
texts.desc: A complex combination of fur and leather used by [Outfitter]s to craft powerful gear.
texts.flavorDesc: This is the animal skin nature chose not to create. It probably had a good reason for this, but human artisans preferred to ignore it.

texts.name: Small Fang
texts.desc: Can be found on forest animals, I guess. It can be used in various jobs.
texts.flavorDesc: Humans no longer make tools with fangs, but they still enjoy collecting them for no particular reason.

texts.name: Nepsid Fin
texts.desc: Fragment of a Nepsid's skin. It can be used in various jobs.
texts.flavorDesc: Nepsids can still swim perfectly without their fins but it makes them look like humans, which is really humiliating.

texts.name: Chipped Tusk
texts.desc: Can be found on wild boars. It can be used in various jobs.
texts.flavorDesc: Initially a good deterrent against rivals and predators, tusks are less advantageous now that they attract poachers.

texts.name: Eyestalk
texts.desc: Almost all creatures have eyes, but this one is found on crabs. It can be used by [Alchemist]s.
texts.flavorDesc: It looks just as lifeless as it did when this crab was still stabbing pirates.

texts.name: Diaphanous Wings
texts.desc: Can be found on bee-like creatures. It can be used by [Blacksmith]s and [Outfitter]s.
texts.flavorDesc: Imagine being able to fly anywhere, and still ending up between the hands of a creature that's stuck to the ground.

texts.name: Cracked Shell
texts.desc: pas encore utilisé
texts.flavorDesc: A sad reminder of the shellfish housing crisis.

texts.name: Mossy Shell
texts.desc: Only the most solid shells can survive long enough to look like that.

texts.name: Fresh Blood
texts.flavorDesc: There is no shortage of blood donors in Siagarta, but most donations are accidental.
texts.desc: Usually found on humanoid creatures. It can be used by [Alchemist]s AND [Cook]s.

texts.name: Thick Blood
texts.flavorDesc: Thick blood is a factor of cardiovascular disease, but it makes a perfect ingredient for cooking, alchemy and black magic.

texts.name: Old Bones
texts.desc: pas encore utilisé
texts.flavorDesc: Old bones are hard to make but pretty easy to break.

texts.name: Curved Claw
texts.desc: pas encore utilisé
texts.flavorDesc: Finding one of these claws is bad luck, especially if it's still on the cat.

texts.name: Razor Claw
texts.flavorDesc: Whoever already got injured by a regular claw has no wish to discover what a "razor claw" is.

texts.name: Scent Gland
texts.desc: pas encore utilisé
texts.flavorDesc: All perfumers know that with great power comes great responsibility.

texts.name: Swollen Gland
texts.desc: This organ is abnormal. No sane creature should have that inside their body, and no sane person should have that between their hands.

texts.name: Fish Oil
texts.desc: Used by [Alchemist]s to craft potions and elixirs.
texts.flavorDesc: A perfect demonstration that the weirder a product smells, the more interesting it is to alchemists.

texts.name: Mote of Pyrh
texts.desc: Can be found on creatures related to fire.
texts.flavorDesc: Whether the 5 elements are humanoid deities, abstract powers or mere chemical types, they can materialize as innocent little specks that agglomerate in golems and sparkles.

texts.name: Fragment of Pyrh
texts.desc: Rarely found on creatures related to fire, it can be used in various jobs.
texts.flavorDesc: Pure incarnation of divine elemental essence, this glowing orb of energy can hardly be described in material terms.

texts.name: Mote of Naya
texts.desc: Can be found on creatures related to water.
texts.flavorDesc: Whether the 5 elements are humanoid deities, abstract powers or mere chemical types, they can materialize as innocent little specks that agglomerate in golems and sparkles.

texts.name: Fragment of Naya
texts.desc: Rarely found on creatures related to water, it can be used in various jobs.

texts.flavorDesc: Whether the 5 elements are humanoid deities, abstract powers or mere chemical types, they can materialize as innocent little specks that agglomerate in golems and sparkles.

texts.desc: Rarely found on creatures related to nature, it can be used in various jobs.

texts.name: Spark Sample
texts.flavorDesc: Pefect association of Luon and Nuon essences, Spark is the source of all life in the 9 Worlds. This sample is the least valuable form of the most valuable existing substance.
texts.desc: This is the base material in crafting; it can be used by all jobs. You can find it by gathering or by completing activities in the world.

texts.name: Spark Powder
texts.desc: Earned from killing outdoor monsters and opening crates. Used to ugprade any kind of item.
texts.flavorDesc: All living creatures have some divine Spark inside them. That makes their killing both morally condemnable and tragically profitable.

texts.name: Nepsid Gland
texts.desc: Earned from killing Nepsids and completing activities. Used to ugprade some gear.
texts.flavorDesc: The exact function of this organ is unknown, but it is speculated to be the cause of the Nepsids' baldness.

texts.name: Infused Teeth
texts.desc: Earned from killing Kobolds and completing activities. Used to ugprade some gear.
texts.flavorDesc: Unlike most other humanoids, Kobolds can renew their teeth. That's not to say they're willing to give them away.

texts.name: Poisonous Sting
texts.desc: Earned from killing Bees and completing activities. Used to ugprade some gear.
texts.flavorDesc: Humans once believed that bees lost their sting after using it. The recent upsurge in bee attacks has disproven these rumors.

texts.name: Sacred Relic
texts.desc: Earned from killing Crimson Rams and completing activities. Used to ugprade some gear.
texts.flavorDesc: This relic is sacred only in the eyes of the Crimson Rams. They are also the only ones who have an idea of what it is.

texts.name: Spark-Infused Crystal
texts.desc: Earned from outdoor Activities. Used to ugprade Rare items up to level 10.
texts.flavorDesc: It's unclear how this crystal got infused with Spark or who cut it so perfectly. But we suspect that the Heralds have something to do with it.

texts.name: Mysterious Cache
texts.desc: Contains something shiny.

texts.flavorDesc:
This is not a proper diary. Through the quiet years of the early Outspring, when each mundane minute of every passing day was worth preserving, these hours felt worth spending in present time rather than pointless reminiscence. Today, for reminiscence has become my only refuge, I am willing to sacrifice a few hours of the dreaded present in hope of approaching one fugitive second of this past. I am, however, only indulging in this ritual in order to regain my strengths. Tomorrow, galvanized by the memory of what once was and the thought of what could be, I will forever abandon these words, take up arms and leave behind me everything I cannot save.


There was never a busier era than the immediate aftermath of the Chrysalis Age. Yet, in those days, our hearts were still full of this very Antelimbian conviction that we had all the time in the world. Change was then a complete stranger to us, as it had been for five centuries. Hence, as we spread across the land of the foremothers, defended ourselves against the demons that had confiscated it and started taking care of the ruins time had left us there, we thought of past and future as one and the same. To us, the land of Siagarta had always been the setting of history and the stage of prophecies. It was both the promise and the origin, the future and the past. Our “present” had merely been the time of waiting and deprivation that stood unnaturally between these two identical states. Now that they had been reunited, there could only be one single, unified era, and we had finally reached it. No other change could ever come, and as eager as we were to taste this new reality, we never imagined that it could be taken from us once more.


I spent these years not knowing who I was and neglecting to care. This question was a meaningless one that could never have entered my mind. To my people, I was queen of Antelimbo, pioneer of Skover and priestess of the Crimson Rams. Leman or Aquilon thought of me as a friend, Serous could count me as an ally and Ashram had chosen me as a spouse. But I had one last and beautiful confirmation that identity is merely something that happens to you, when little Talitha made a mother of me, thus creating the last and most fortunate Queenevere Auroram to join the long cohort of my aliases. These were the faces I wore day after day, from the most public to the most intimate. Only now that all these beloved masks have been shattered can I understand the meaning of “who am I?” Standing before the disseminated remains of these lost identities, I am facing a choice for the very first time, and what I make of it will be my answer. Which part of this broken reality will I fight to restore? Which hope will I desperately dedicate all my time to? Which ghost will I run after once I let down my quill and abandon this booklet?


As far as I am concerned, Antelimbo can sink and Skover can wait. The Crimson Rams will one fateful day finish to dissolve themselves in their grotesque hatred. Aquilon is chasing a dream I do not share. Leman is preserving a barren tradition I had already forgotten about many lives ago. Serous is standing by my side, and their faith might help me reach that distant beam of redeeming light, the only hope my soul can still fathom: the possibility that Talitha is still alive and waiting for someone to bring her back. There is no doubt Ashram’s spirit will accompany me as I neglect his memory to honor this higher pursuit, for I know even his latest acts of madness were merely his own, ill-fated attempts at reaching it. Now that he has failed, I cannot afford to.
texts.name: Late Spring Diary
texts.author: Queenevere Auroram

texts.flavorDesc:
“Should a criminal lay bare their actions on paper?” Such a cowardly thought could only emerge from the constipated mind of an obedient Crimson stooge. To speak such dry cognitions is to admit your brains are some bowel-shaped ouroboros that can only ever digest what it has already digested over and over again until its substance just evaporates. What if writing things down helps me process, you ever think of that? Besides, what wrong could bragging do me if you people already know very well what I did and never caught me yet? Now, I got y’all thinking, didn’t I? That’s the way it is. The more you know, the more scared you are, and the freer I become. So I might as well tell y’all everything. I might as well confess the crimes I didn’t have time to commit yet or the ones others pulled off and I wished I did instead, don’t you think? Well, that’s not like I’m really interested in what you think anyway, so it’s good you can’t answer me at all.


Now, that nice little story of the pirate renaissance as people like to call it, where does it start exactly? I could go all the way back to the old Kiara Vel and all those freebooters of ancient times who had it quite good as it looks like, or I could ramble about Opal Myra and the Floating Nation or whatnot, but the truth is I know nothing about all that stuff, so let’s not pretend otherwise. It would make more sense to recount the travels of Captain Narval and Barkbarossa the dog captain and how they met in the nasty quods of Merops, but honestly that’s none of my business so I’ll keep it simple and speak only of what I’ve seen with my own eyes. Also, that should keep things much shorter, and being no intellectual I must say I’m already fed up with all this writing.


When I first encountered the proud pirates of Siagarta, it’s no secret that I sunk one or two of their ships and that I continued to behave in a less than friendly manner for quite some time, I’m not ashamed to say it. But if you need proof that they’re a bunch of jolly good fellows, just remember they never held a grudge against me for spending like ten years drowning them and dismembering them and putting dozens of them on fire. Even more impressively, they don’t look down on me for having been with the Crimson Rams for so long. That's what I call respect and with the Rams, you don’t get that. Especially now that they’ve stooped to obeying this despicable desiccated beetroot who likes to call himself Chakram while I personally prefer to call him Inquisitor Buttocks, at least you know what you get. No illusions about what comes out of that dirty mouth. We pirates got a reputation for bad language but with all these old rotten words we love, we get to talk about nice, lovely things. Whereas with this guy, it’s just the opposite. Think of that what you will.


Anyway, when my old comrade King Ashram who had frankly become quite a nutcase finally kicked the bucket, there was no reason for me to keep working with this lot of spineless maniacs, which honestly I had only been suffering out of friendship and loyalty. So I asked the pirates for a job and it turned out an admiral was precisely what they needed (I made my own bed here cause I had just wasted a few of theirs). Besides, we were in the same line of work and all I needed to become one of them was a change of clothes. I soon found out they were having much more fun on this side of the little quarrel we'd been waging. Except now that the Crimson no longer had an admiral we could fight, and since the best of my men had followed me, things started to get boring very soon. That’s around that time Barkbarossa had his most exciting idea yet.


The thing is, the old man – who’s actually some kind of dog but when you’ve met the man, you’ll never call him that to his face – he was getting fairly old, and he saw very well that this little routine of plundering the Rams’ routes was getting old too. There were quite a bunch of us now and we needed something more interesting to do, which Barkbarossa was too tired to steer. So he offered us a deal: we had five admirals (there had been six at one point but I whacked one too many and then I couldn’t replace them all). All we had to do was to send each one to a different region, with a fifth of our ships and a fifth of our men, and then we’d see the world a little and have more scoundrels to recruit and honest citizens to rob. That was a perfect plan and as a matter of fact, we’re leaving tomorrow. The only thing left to decide at that point was, if there are just five of us and there are six regions: which one of these regions would have to survive without pirates? Since we’re all fed up with Skover and know nothing about the five other places, we unanimously decided to leave and abandon the Rams to their boring lives.
texts.name: Chronicles of the Pirate Renaissance
texts.author: Admiral Eleanor d'Aquilon

texts.flavorDesc:
You say “who is that bogeyman Amon Ram we have never seen or even heard about?” And I say it is not necessary for Amon Ram to manifest itself through physical form, for their truth is transcendent enough to penetrate one’s soul directly. I am not making this up. As you absorb my words, your soul is already impregnated with such truth, which may feel gross but it has always been like that and we all have to get used to it. Yet, as your ignorance demonstrates, it is easy to spend one’s entire life remaining oblivious to that truth within one’s soul. Many stupid folks have done that – and I know these were your parents and grandparents and whatnot but that does not make them any more enlightened – as it requires genuine, deliberate openness to recognize and receive truth. Let me describe that truth as it appeared to me when I opened my perception to pure, unfiltered vision.


With my eyes closed, I beheld the small but tightly assembled number of the surviving humans putting all their efforts in a mysterious craft. Blindly following the instructions of their guide, they sculpted with much patience and little skill what looked like bizarre headpieces. Then, one by one, as they completed these creations, they proudly lifted and wore the headpieces over their heads, revealing them to be sets of humble makeshift horns. Once they were all done doing that, they gathered around their guide, who was then revealed to be Amon Ram in flesh and bones. The humans formed a large square as they waited for the guide’s words.


“One day will come”, Amon said, “when your people shall rise up once more and deserve actual horns that do not make you all look so helpless. And it shall take three decades for your fate to unfold and for your offspring to rise up and for their horns to point towards the sky.” Everyone listened in silence, and I said nothing either because I was not there. “In the first decade of this new age, a path shall be revealed that will lead your people to the land of your sacred grandmothers. When this day comes, will your offspring remember that land?” They had to interrupt themselves as the humans started answering. “For Luon’s sake that was a rhetorical question, why are you saying “yes”? You know very well these poor clowns will have spent their entire lives here in this timeforsaken backwater. How would they remember the land of your sacred grandmothers? Now, stay quiet and listen. What was I saying? That’s right. First decade: path revealed. Then, in the second decade of this new age, a martyr shall emerge to revive the ancient strife that once united the Sunlings.”


But the humans did not seem to understand. Amon Ram contemplated them in silence and disbelief for a brief moment, after which they resumed their speech: “This is your people I am talking about, here. You are the bloody Sunlings! And you could show more enthusiasm when I reveal that a martyr will emerge from your ranks. Whatever! Let us just go on with the prophecy… That martyr shall remind you that there is no great unity without a great rival. And your horns shall meet the horns of the noble Nightlings. Then, in the fratricide fight against the horned Nightlings, the martyr shall reveal their boundless commitment to the great unity. And within this unity, their life shall be absorbed. Which means they will die, I would rather say it plainly so you do not misquote me later. Finally, in the third decade of this new age, a new prophet shall emerge from the shadow of the lost martyr. This one shall become your true sovereign, recognized as a direct successor of the glory that once was. Now, what do you think about that?” As the humans remained silent, Amon Ram observed them again. Then, with an expression of indecipherable melancholy, the guide turned their back on the bewitched assistance and disappeared as they began to cheer, shouting glory to the Ram and striking their wooden horns with sticks.
texts.name: Thus Spoke Amon Ram
texts.author: Lady Majoram Skover

texts.flavorDesc:
Nandat Silean, Quamel cycle of Year 1089: Early observations

Five centuries have passed since the Sunlings allowed a cohort of glorified imps to bully them into the most forlorn corner of the world. Following this disaster, we endured an age of pathetic introspection, as we were left to ponder how the glorious legions of King Regate and his 6 Suzerains – which had proved capable of trampling in a matter of days the millennial defenses of the Nine Worlds – let themselves be subjugated by the old tricks of a few disorganized Nightlings. Now, as we face these obscene ghouls once again, the answer becomes increasingly clear: our understanding of the Nightlings’ Sigils is insufficient. These are the only weapons in their possession we are still unable to replicate or properly protect ourselves against. I consider it my duty, as the legitimate successor of King Regate on the ancestors’ land, to repair this ancient injustice by seizing the Nuon magic from the Nightlings’ barbaric hands. To this end, my preferred methods will be trickery, torture and vivisection.


The first weeks of my research have already yielded surprising results. Namely, it appeared to me that the Nightlings do not merely use those Sigils as enchantments, but also as protections against all kinds of threats, as keys guarding the passage between two worlds and – most bafflingly – as some sort of primitive language. In that regard, their magic seems to mirror that of the Heralds. Therefore, since those winged hypocrites are so reluctant to share the essence of their knowledge, we shall extract such knowledge more efficiently by using strength on the Nightlings than prayers on the Heralds. I much prefer this new strategy to the fruitless, humiliating years we spent begging their insufferable envoy ul’Serous to tell us anything of their petty secrets. Now, the presence of that useless scoundrel will no longer be tolerated inside the Citadel, and knowing the poor angel will sleep outside our gates on this stormy night fills me with the utmost satisfaction.


Lately, in the name of an ill-inspired morality, the weakest and most corrupt of our ministers have tried preventing me from cutting live Nightlings in half. Were these spoilsports still alive, they would be humiliated by the tremendous results this practice has already returned. During the last cycle alone, we were able to observe that the demons’ bodies are protected by multiple layers of curse. The number of those seems to depend on the subject’s rank in their aristocratic hierarchy. Each Nightlord bears a specific Sigil, which they seemingly use as a makeshift heraldry. One would argue that studying the customs of such a decadent people is a waste of time, but I must confess that I increasingly find the Nightlords to be much more interesting and worthy than the depraved human factions we have to tolerate for now. I respect their heathen attempts at attaining greatness through strength and hierarchy more than I can support the sophisticated weakness of the boneless Pilgrims and subservient Silkworms.
texts.name: Notes on the Use and Extraction of Niflelian Magic

texts.name: Sparkstone
texts.desc: Return to the last Obelisk you set as a Teleportation point.
texts.flavorDesc: Taken from the remains of an ancient Gate, this stone is what the Obelisks were made from. It connects them to the Spark flows going through space and time.

texts.name: Vial
texts.desc: Used by [Alchemist]s to craft potions and elixirs.

texts.name: Simple Cauldron
texts.desc: Can be used by [Alchemist]s.

texts.name: Gem Powder
texts.desc: Some gem powder useful to [Jeweller]s.

texts.name: Small Alchemist Cauldron
texts.desc: Can be used by [Alchemist]s.

texts.name: Hess Toss Flask
texts.desc:
The [RefillableFlask] is a powerful potion that almost instantly revigorates the body upon consumption.
It can be refilled at the Obelisks' waters.

texts.name: Minor Healing Potion
texts.desc: Regenerate 100 [Health] over 10s.

texts.name: Minor Shield Potion
texts.desc: Gain a Shield absorbing 150 damage for 20s.

texts.name: Health Potion
texts.desc: Regenerate 300 [Health] over 10s.

texts.name: Minor Velocity Potion
texts.desc: Increase your movement speed by 50% for 10s.

texts.name: Long Breath Potion
texts.desc: Increases your breath under water for 2mn.

texts.name: Fast Swim Potion
texts.desc: While under water, you move 30% faster for 20s.

texts.name: Invisibility Potion
texts.desc: Gain invisiblity for 15s.

texts.desc:
Increase your [Strength] for one hour.

You can only have one Elixir at a time.

texts.name: Elixir of Minor Dexterity

You can only have one Elixir at a time.

texts.desc: Increase your chance to gather rare materials in the world for one hour. Remains after death.

texts.name: Wolf Meat
texts.desc: Can be eaten raw or used by [Cook]s.
texts.flavorDesc: "Inside you there are two wolves." Thay saying takes on a whole different meaning after a wolf meat feast.

texts.name: Skunk Meat
texts.desc: Can be eaten raw or used by [Cook]s.
texts.flavorDesc: Skunk meat doesn't smell funny. What you're perceiving is the hunter's perfume.

texts.name: Boar Meat
texts.desc: Can be eaten raw or used by [Cook]s.
texts.flavorDesc: The taste of boar meat depends on the animal's diet. For instance, hunting in areas where slimes are abundant is not recommended.

texts.name: Fleshy Claw
texts.flavorDesc: Easy to pick up, hard to shatter. Foragers usually get less energy from this tiny bit of flesh than they spent trying to break its shell.
texts.desc: Can be eaten raw or used by [Cook]s.

texts.name: Big Fleshy Claw
texts.desc: Easy to pick up, hard to shatter. Foragers usually get less energy from this tiny bit of flesh than they spent trying to break its shell.

texts.name: Small Mackerel
texts.desc: Can be eaten raw or used by [Cook]s.
texts.flavorDesc: Back in the confused times of the Lost Gods Age, this species was known as the Fox Trout.

texts.name: Pike
texts.desc: Can be eaten raw or used by [Cook]s.
texts.flavorDesc: Back in the confused times of the Lost Gods Age, this species was known as the Drumen Bass.

texts.name: Honey
texts.desc: Can be eaten raw or used by [Cook]s.
texts.flavorDesc: This resource was much easier to come by back when the bees didn't have an army.

texts.name: Small Cheese
texts.desc: Can be eaten raw or used by [Cook]s.
texts.flavorDesc: That cheese is only produced by the Kobolds in the underground valleys of Irukal, with the milk of their domestic aurocks.

texts.name: Old Cheese
texts.flavorDesc: A Kobold could kill for such a delicacy. Actually, there's a high chance a Kobold did kill for that cheese at one point.

texts.name: Plainswalker Feast
texts.flavorDesc: The Pilgrims of Merilam are not keen on farming but they excel at foraging and bartering, which makes their feasts varied and surprising.

texts.name: Wild Boar Stew
texts.desc:
Can be eaten to restore Health and increase attributes.

You can only have one Food at a time.
texts.flavorDesc: First discovered by a Pilgrim scout in an abandoned Nightling camp, this recipe might be the only trace we have of Nightling cuisine.

texts.name: Grilled Wolf Chops
texts.desc:
Can be eaten to restore Health and increase attributes.

You can only have one Food at a time.
texts.flavorDesc: The Crimson Rams aren't known for their cooking skills, but they're known for killing many wolves and starting many fires, which eventually led to the invention of this staple dish.

texts.name: Crab Goulash
texts.desc:
Can be eaten to restore Health and increase attributes.

You can only have one Food at a time.
texts.flavorDesc: There's not much left from the time Barkbarossa's pirates spent in Skover, but their culinary legacy lives on among the populations they used to terrify.

texts.name: Twice-Boiled Perch
texts.desc:
Can be eaten to restore Health and increase attributes.

You can only have one Food at a time.
texts.flavorDesc: Invented during the great fish poisoning of the Chrysalis Age, this recipe lived on despite the significant loosening up of sanitary precautions surrounding fish.

texts.name: Grilled Mackerel
texts.desc:
Can be eaten to restore Health and increase attributes.

You can only have one Food at a time.
texts.flavorDesc: Imported by the Nepsids from their native Merops, this recipe requires more skill than ingredients. They find it convenient when invading territories where the usual resources are unavailable.

texts.name: Honey-marinated Skunk
texts.desc:
Can be eaten to restore Health and increase attributes.

You can only have one Food at a time.
texts.flavorDesc: Among its various attempts at mimicking humanoid behavior, the Apix Clan developed a few cooking techniques that are starting to exert a surprising influence.

texts.name: Flavored Candies
texts.desc:
Can be eaten to restore Health and increase attributes.

You can only have one Food at a time.
texts.flavorDesc: When travelling between worlds, the merchants of the 7 Circles Guild need lightweight and non-perishable food to carry around. Candies combine these practical qualities with aesthetical and gustative ones.

texts.name: Mixed Salad
texts.desc:
Can be eaten to restore Health and increase attributes.

You can only have one Food at a time.
texts.flavorDesc: The hard-working artisans of the Silkworm Fellowship have little time to cook or eat. The solution they came up with is this nourishing yet practical recipe.

texts.name: Dripping Debris
texts.desc: It looks like gooey dirt, and that's probably what it is, but there's something shiny underneath it all.

texts.name: Scroll Of Strength I
texts.flavorDesc: Now that literary scrolls can improve combat abilities, the distinction between nerdy scholars and brutal bullies is becoming increasingly porous.

texts.desc: Increases your [PhysicalMastery] if you are equipped with a sharp weapon.
texts.flavorDesc: Without a good sharpening tool such as this one, even the most legendary sword is hardly more dangerous than a mere theater prop.

texts.name: Weightstone
texts.desc: Increases your [PhysicalMastery] if you are equipped with a blunt weapon.
texts.flavorDesc: In a strongman hammer game, carrying this stone would be considered cheating. On a battlefield, however, anything goes.

texts.name: Training Manual
texts.desc: Gain a weapon skill rank.

texts.flavorDesc: It may look like a slice of ham destined to fit in an armor sandwich. But in the heat of battle, this is the only kind of filling that could save one's life.

texts.flavorDesc: This mysterious stone has fascinating properties. Mounted on a piece of equipment, it makes its bearer more dangerous. But carried inside a merchant's bag, it makes them a vulnerable target.

texts.flavorDesc: Beryls reveal the true potential of everything they touch. They turn impurities into vibrant hues, jeweller cuts into magical power, and average adventurers into heroic warriors.

texts.name: Small Pouch
texts.desc: Increases your inventory size by 6 slots.
texts.flavorDesc: Small enough to feel weightless but big enough to fit heavy stuff inside.

texts.name: Linen Bag
texts.desc: Increases your inventory size by 8 slots.
texts.flavorDesc: Ideal for wanderers travelling light or for hoarders adding a 16th compartment to their heavy backpack.

texts.name: Enripian Wolf
texts.desc: Increase your movement speed in the world by 80%.
texts.flavorDesc: In the vast plains of Enripit, the fastest way to travel is to tame a wild wolf. It might also be the most dangerous and the least comfortable way, but style comes at a price.

texts.name: Nescentine Wolf
texts.desc: Increase your movement speed in the world by 80%.
texts.flavorDesc: In the bountiful fields of Nescent, wolf mounts are favored over goats and donkeys because there's no risk that they'll eat the crops.

texts.name: Krisomalese Wolf
texts.desc: Increase your movement speed in the world by 80%.
texts.flavorDesc: In the desolate ruins of Krisomal, wolves were trained to sniff out treasures hidden under the debris. Sadly, this one lost the sense of smell after a violent encounter with a skunk.

texts.name: Navelian Wolf
texts.desc: Increase your movement speed in the world by 80%.
texts.flavorDesc: In the Pilgrim village of Navelin, taming wolves from the nearby woods is just another way of preventing them from devouring careless adventurers.

texts.name: Almazean Wolf
texts.desc: Increase your movement speed in the world by 80%.
texts.flavorDesc: On the snowy plateau of Almaz, wolves used to have a hard time finding enough prey. That all changed when the Guild started sending merchants through the area.

texts.name: Sforian Wolf
texts.desc: Increase your movement speed in the world by 80%.
texts.flavorDesc: In the dark forests of Sforia, there's no road wide enough for a chariot, and no road safe enough for a leggybug. That makes wolves the only rational means of transportation.

texts.name: Fountrailian Hound
texts.desc: todo
texts.flavorDesc: By the torrential rivers of Fountrail, hounds cannot climb the mountain paths as fast as goats do, but their fur keeps the travelers warm, and their teeth keep the travelers safe.

texts.name: Skoverial Hound
texts.desc: todo
texts.flavorDesc: In the forgotten land of Skover, hounds are often associated with the Crimson Rams faction, but they are recommended companions for pretty much everyone.

texts.name: Antelimbian Hound
texts.desc: todo
texts.flavorDesc: In the endless autumn of Antelimbo, hounds are valued for their orange robe reminiscent of fallen leaves.

texts.name: Ramburgian Hound
texts.desc: todo
texts.flavorDesc: In the Crimson city of Ramburg, hounds are prized as a status symbol. A deep red robe indicates that the hound's master is a bloodthirsty monster, even by the Crimsons' standards.

texts.name: Lemian Hound
texts.desc: todo
texts.flavorDesc: In the arid plains of Lema, the pale robe of this hound allows it to remain undetected. Which is pretty useless in a desertic area where nothing would detect it in the first place.

texts.name: Niflelian Hound
texts.desc: todo
texts.flavorDesc: In the forbidden underworld of Niflel, hounds are very similar to the ones we find in Siagarta. They only differ in that they're filled with the Nuon essence of chaos.

texts.name: Ebralean Goat
texts.desc: todo
texts.flavorDesc: On the craggy paths of Ebral, the safest way to travel is to ride ibexes. Their nimbleness is an inspiration to the Crimson Rams, who remain the worst mountaineers in Siagarta.

texts.name: Kondalese Goat
texts.desc: todo
texts.flavorDesc: On the rocky ledges of Konda, humans have tried taking advantage of the goats' spectacular climbing skills. Unfortunately, the poor animals found climbing much harder with humans on their backs.

texts.name: Opaline Goat
texts.desc: todo
texts.flavorDesc: On the luxurious island of Opal, goats were once bred as farm animals, but have returned to the wild after the humans left. This one, however, didn't have that chance.

texts.name: Crimson Goat
texts.desc: todo
texts.flavorDesc: Inside the well-guarded Crimson Citadel, the Crimson Rams revere goats as sacred animals. That doesn't stop the clan from exploiting them, riding them or even eating them.

texts.name: Zerzurean Goat
texts.desc: todo
texts.flavorDesc: In the blazing highworld of Zerzur, goats were introduced as an exotic curiosity after the Guardians War, but proved so popular they eventually became a symbol of local culture.

texts.name: Niflelian Goat
texts.desc: todo
texts.flavorDesc: In the forbidden underworld of Niflel, goats are admired for their spectacular horns and inscrutable eyes, which reminds the Nightlings of their beloved mothers.

texts.name: Meridional Hog
texts.desc: todo
texts.flavorDesc: In the lush forests of Meridion, the most efficient way to travel is not to run fast, but to avoid running into foes. In that regard, the intimidating stare of the hog is more useful than the buttontail's sprint.

texts.name: Jodarian Hog
texts.desc: todo
texts.flavorDesc: In the lost megapolis of Jodar, hogs existed before the humans settled in, accompanied all eras of their local history, and still subsist to this day.

texts.name: Alandian Hog
texts.desc: todo
texts.flavorDesc: By the mystical river Alandal, breeders like to think their hogs will be blessed if they swim daily in the cold water. Blessed or not, the exercise makes them stronger.

texts.name: Irukalean Hog
texts.desc: todo
texts.flavorDesc: In the hollow underworld of Irukal, the locals have been breeding Aurocks for millenia, but only recently introduced the boar as a draught animal.

texts.name: Veilantine Hog
texts.desc: todo
texts.flavorDesc: In the scorched region of Veilant, most of the wildlife has disappeared, but hogs are still bred as warmounts to guard the old Merilam.

texts.name: Niflelian Hog
texts.desc: todo
texts.flavorDesc: In the forbidden underworld of Niflel, all creatures are infused with Nuon essence. In the case of hogs, this has absolutely no effect other than a change in color.

texts.name: Primevallean Skunk
texts.desc: todo
texts.flavorDesc: On the verdant glens of Primevalley, skunks were formerly seen as a nuisance. That was before tamers found out the smell problem could be fixed by feeding them only flowers.

texts.name: Sperian Skunk
texts.desc: todo
texts.flavorDesc: In the abandoned land of Spero, skunks have been using the ruins as shelter for centuries, which made them less reluctant to move into barns when tamers started capturing them.

texts.name: Crosselian Skunk
texts.desc: todo
texts.flavorDesc: By the diverging rivers of Crossroam, there are too many merchants and not enough mounts to meet the demand, which led to skunks being domesticated.

texts.name: Rinuhrian Skunk
texts.desc: todo
texts.flavorDesc: In the fertile gorges of Rinuhr, many animals gather to avoid the surrounding drought. In this overcrowded area, only the skunks are able to keep everyone out of their preferred spots.

texts.name: Ponogian Skunk
texts.desc: todo
texts.flavorDesc: In the rainy groundworld of Ponog, the odoriferous power of skunks is mostly neutralized. For this reason, it is the only region where they are the preferred means of transportation.

texts.name: Niflelian Skunk
texts.desc: todo
texts.flavorDesc: In the forbidden underworld of Niflel, skunks were accidentally introduced when they started crossing the Sundering to feed on stagflowers.

texts.name: Eksodean Crocoboar
texts.desc: todo
texts.flavorDesc: On the steep trails of Eksod, merchants often have to abandon their mounts in favor of mountain-proof goats. As a result, there is a growing number of wild crocoboars in the area.

texts.name: Ruleanese Crocoboar
texts.desc: todo
texts.flavorDesc: In the windy archipelago of Rulean, crocoboars are an endemic species that used to cause many problems. The locals solved this by selling these aggressive creatures as pets across Siagarta.

texts.name: Beltirian Crocoboar
texts.desc: todo
texts.flavorDesc: On the cursed cliffs of Bel-Etir, there are so many hostile creatures that crocoboars are seen by humans as a reassuring presence.

texts.name: Egheretrian Crocoboar
texts.desc: todo
texts.flavorDesc: By the clear river Egheret, crocoboars can enjoy the two things they enjoy most: fresh water and extreme drought.

texts.name: Atlanese Crocoboar
texts.desc: todo
texts.flavorDesc: In the forbidden underworld of Niflel, human legends say that the rivers are made of blood. This belief only stems from the intriguing color of their crocoboars.

texts.name: Niflelian Crocoboar
texts.desc: todo
texts.flavorDesc: In the forbidden underworld of Niflel, human legends say that the rivers are made of blood. This belief only stems from the intriguing color of their crocoboars.

texts.name: Leggy Buggy
texts.desc: todo
texts.flavorDesc: Originally bred by farmers to rid their crops of midges, leggybugs eventually grew big enough to be employed as draught animals, and later as recreational vehicles.
